observations: ct-service-card renders no images/icons of its own — the decorative top stripe is a pure-CSS ::before with no informational content needing an alternative. Any non-text content in the content-top/content-bottom slots is that slotted content's own responsibility.

standard: 1.2.3 Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded) (Level A)

observations: The title is always rendered through a real ct-heading (a semantic <h4>) rather than styled text, and links are composed via ct-item-list, which renders a real <ul>/<li> structure. Establishing correct heading and list semantics for whatever the implementer supplies is this component's entire purpose here, so it's met unconditionally rather than depending on the implementer to add it separately.

content-top, the title, links, and content-bottom render in a fixed DOM order that matches the visual stacked layout; no CSS reordering (order/flex-direction: row-reverse, etc.) is used anywhere in the component.
The card is a fluid width: 100% block with no fixed aspect ratio or orientation lock.
ct-service-card renders no form inputs.
The theme-driven background and top stripe are decorative differentiators only; the title and link text remain the actual carriers of meaning and are never conveyed by color alone.

The card's own background-color tokens (--ct-service-card-light/dark-background-color) are the standard CivicTheme surface colors, verified against the composed ct-heading/ct-link's own theme-matched text-color tokens. Compliance depends on the implementer keeping `theme` consistent between ct-service-card and anything slotted into content-top/content-bottom, since a mismatched theme on slotted content isn't something this component can enforce.
Padding and border-radius are rem-based and no fixed height or overflow: hidden is applied, so the card grows to fit its content when text is resized to 200%.
ct-service-card renders no text of its own outside the title, which is always real text via ct-heading.

The card has no fixed width or horizontal-scroll container; its content padding and the vertical stacking of content-top/title/links/content-bottom all reflow normally down to a 320px-equivalent viewport.

The card's own visual elements (background, top stripe, box-shadow) are decorative container styling, not UI components/graphical objects requiring a 3:1 contrast ratio. Contrast for any actual interactive control (a slotted ct-link) is that component's own responsibility.

ct-service-card sets no line-height, letter-spacing, or word-spacing overrides of its own and imposes no fixed-height/overflow:hidden container that could clip re-spaced text in the composed title or slotted content.

standard: 1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus (Level AA)

observations: The only interactive content the component's own markup can introduce is whatever the implementer slots into `links` — real ct-link elements rendering native <a> tags, keyboard-operable by default. ct-service-card itself introduces no custom keyboard handling.

standard: 2.3.1 Three Flashes or Below Threshold (Level A)

observations: A single service card is not a repeated block of navigation/content requiring its own skip mechanism; that's a page-level concern for whatever lists these cards.

As with 1.3.2, DOM order (content-top, title, links, content-bottom) is never reassigned via CSS order, so any focusable elements slotted in receive focus in an order matching both the DOM and the visual layout.

ct-service-card renders no links of its own — link purpose depends entirely on the implementer providing descriptive `label` text on each ct-link slotted into `links` (e.g. "Collection schedule", not "Click here").

Compliance depends on the implementer providing a clear, descriptive `title` — the composed ct-heading renders whatever text is passed verbatim at a fixed level (4), with no wording validation of its own.
Focus styling for any slotted ct-link is inherited from that component's own :focus-visible outline (--ct-outline-* tokens); ct-service-card overrides no focus treatment.
The card has no sticky/fixed-position elements of its own that could obscure a focused descendant.
ct-service-card renders no accessible-name-bearing interactive controls of its own; any slotted ct-link's accessible name is that component's own responsibility.
ct-service-card defines no pointer targets of its own; target size for a slotted ct-link is that component's own baseline hit area, unmodified by any padding override here.
Page language is set on the html element, not by this component.
Compliance depends on the implementer setting a lang attribute on the title or slotted content when its language differs from the page's.
Focusing a link within the card triggers no context change of the card's own doing.
ct-service-card has no input props that trigger behavior — `theme`/`title`/`modifierClass` only affect rendering, never navigation or context.
Compliance depends on the implementer using the same title/links composition pattern for structurally-equivalent service cards wherever they recur across a listing page.
ct-service-card renders no form inputs.
ct-service-card is a content card, not a form control; it provides no labels or instructions of its own.

The composed ct-heading and any slotted ct-item-list/ct-link expose correct native roles (heading, list, listitem, link) automatically with no custom ARIA needed; ct-service-card's own root is a non-interactive <div> requiring no role of its own.
ct-service-card is static content with no live-updating behavior or state changes of its own to announce.
